The Swipe Files
of the
Copywriting
SuperSTarS
Brad
petersen

-- 1 of 32 --

The Swipe Files of the Copywriting Superstars
Brad
PETERSEN
Testimonials:
“Brad’s so reliable I even coughed up a six-figure annual retainer to keep him locked up for years.”
-- Clayton Makepeace
Copywriter & Publisher, The Total Package
“Brad’s been a popular fixture at a number of my marketing seminars. And the copy he’s produced
for both my seminar attendees and for me has always been top-notch.”
-- Gary Halbert
Editor, The Gary Halbert Letter, Miami, FL
“The insert you wrote for my dad’s book was the most successful in our newsletter’s history. We
recently turned it into a sales letter and on some lists it’s pulling 7%.”
-- John King
Future Economic Trends, Santa Barbara, CA
“I was so locked into ‘efficiency mode’ on the phone that I forgot to tell you what a great job you did.
It makes my life a lot easier to know that the copy I get from you will be right on target. Thanks for an
excellent job.”
-- Bob Serling
VP of Marketing, Nforma, Inc., San Diego, CA
“Your ads and sales letters have helped us sell over $3,000,000 (wholesale) worth of cues in just two
years. We’re doing far better than I ever expected and are now the #1 cue in our price category.”
-- Jim Lucas
Players Cues, Jacksonville, FL
Contact Info:
1840 S. Elena Avenue, Suite 203
Redondo Beach, CA 90277
Daytime Phone: (310) 540-9829
Bio:
An 18-year veteran of direct marketing, Brad got his start working for industry legends Robert J.
Ringer and Jay Abraham in the 1980s. In 1990, he moved on to a full-time freelance career and has
since written controls for some of the largest mailers in the business, including Weiss Publishing,
Phillips Publishing and Agora, Inc.
He’s also written profitable ads and sales letters for a variety of smaller companies in a diverse ar-
ray of industries, including an insurance agency, a pool cue manufacturer and distributor, plumbing
and HVAC contractors, specialty publishers, a money management firm, a commodities broker, and
many others.

of 2003-2004

by:
Brad Petersen
